sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the charcoal box didn't change the board (board_changed: false). Let me think about this differently.
Current state:
- Left green box (NWN): at cols 27-29, still movable with LEFT/RIGHT
- Right green box (NBN): at cols 42-44, locked (past purple bar)
- White square: at cols 30-32
- Purple bar: at cols 33-38
The right green box is near the right charcoal box but not inside it. Let me think about what the goal could be.
Looking at the visual layout again:
- Left panel: charcoal frame + green box with white center
- Middle area: green box with black center + white square
- Purple bar (separator)
- Right panel: charcoal frame
Maybe the goal is to move both green boxes into their respective charcoal frames? The left green box needs to go LEFT to reach the left charcoal frame, and the right green box needs to be positioned to reach the right charcoal frame.
But the right green box is already past the purple bar and doesn't move with RIGHT anymore. Can it move with LEFT? Let me try LEFT to see if the right-side box moves left.
Actually, I should first reset to the initial state to understand the mechanics better. Let me press LEFT 4 times to get the left box back to its original position and see what happens to the right box.
Wait, but the right box is already past the purple bar. If I press LEFT, will it move the right box back left? Let me try.
